mapper.sql: id, order_no, demand_id, quote_id, client_id, developer_id, order_title, amount_total, escrow_amount, platform_fee, pay_status, status, current_stage_no, progress_percent, selected_at, paid_at, started_at, submitted_at, accepted_at, completed_at, cancelled_at, cancel_reason, version, created_at, updated_at
mapper.insert: insert into `orders` ( order_no, demand_id, quote_id, client_id, developer_id, order_title, amount_total, escrow_amount, platform_fee, pay_status, status, current_stage_no, progress_percent, selected_at, paid_at, started_at, submitted_at, accepted_at, completed_at, cancelled_at, cancel_reason, version, created_at, updated_at ) values ( #{orderNo}, #{demandId}, #{quoteId}, #{clientId}, #{developerId}, #{orderTitle}, #{amountTotal}, #{escrowAmount}, #{platformFee}, #{payStatus}, #{status}, #{currentStageNo}, #{progressPercent}, #{selectedAt}, #{paidAt}, #{startedAt}, #{submittedAt}, #{acceptedAt}, #{completedAt}, #{cancelledAt}, #{cancelReason}, #{version}, #{createdAt}, #{updatedAt} )
mapper.select: select from `orders` where client_id = #{clientId} order by id desc
select from `orders` where developer_id = #{developerId} order by id desc

select from `orders`
and status = #{status}
and ( order_no like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') or order_title like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') )
order by id desc

select count(1) from `orders` where status in
#{status}
mapper.update: update `orders` set pay_status = 1, status = 2, paid_at = #{paidAt}, updated_at = now() where id = #{id} and client_id = #{clientId} and status = 1
update `orders` set status = 3, started_at = #{startedAt}, progress_percent = 5, updated_at = now() where id = #{id} and developer_id = #{developerId} and status = 2

update `orders` set status = 5, pay_status = 2, accepted_at = #{acceptedAt}, completed_at = #{completedAt}, progress_percent = 100, updated_at = now() where id = #{id} and client_id = #{clientId} and current_stage_no = #{currentStageNo} and status = 4
update `orders` set status = 7, updated_at = now() where id = #{id} and status in (2, 3, 4)
update `orders` set status = #{status}, pay_status = #{payStatus}, updated_at = now() where id = #{id} and status = 7
